benchmark_result.review_text: I had an amazing experience with Ms. Tracy Kinney. She thoroughly reviewed over 50,000 pages of records and pointed out for the judge which page gave an example summary. She was very knowledgeable and to the point. She seemed to know the judge and exactly what she was looking for. She called me the day before the hearing and answered all my questions. After the hearing she followed up with a call to see if I had any additional questions. All in all I was very impressed!

themes.sentence_snippet: …ad questions they answered them within a day. Felt a little impersonal as my contact changed multiple times, I believe m…

benchmark_result.review_text: They were very good at returning your call. When you had questions they answered them within a day. Felt a little impersonal as my contact changed multiple times, I believe my atty did too. I had a few surprises at the end as those items were not discussed but nothing that mattered when I found out as they were Social security "rules". I received my determination/settlement from Social Security and did not hear from them until a month later. I think they should tell you that no matter how long you wait for your disability you are not likely to get any more than 18 months of benefits. That info is buried in your paperwork from the SS office with your settlement. They did get it done! Glad it is over.

themes.sentence_snippet: …ked with Kong (not sure that is spelled right). He was very understanding and helped me through paperwork. Then, the den…

benchmark_result.review_text: I guess my experience with them may not be complete, but from what I have experienced so far I am frustrated. I went through them to apply for social security disability. I worked with Kong (not sure that is spelled right). He was very understanding and helped me through paperwork. Then, the denial came 3 months later, something he told me to expect. I called him. He said he would send my case to the appeals department and they would call me the next day or two. About 5 days later, I called them to see when the appeals was planning on calling. The person said they had tried to call me that morning—no message was left. I seldom answer a number I don't recognize and figure if it is important they will leave a message. The call representative said she would pass my message on to "her". It is 24 hours later and no call. It is very frustrating because there is a date on the letter and appeals have to be done within the time frame allowed. I would have given Kong a 5 star rating, but overall it is a 3. If there was a 2 1/2 I would have done that. I kind of wish I had gone to another lawyer. UPDATE: I have a new case manager now. She seems to be conscientious regarding my case. I seldom reach her on the first call, but she usually returns my call by the next day or day after.

I was frustrated in dealing with Hoglund Law regarding my Social Security disability. Lack of communication on their end. The lawyer was way better than the paralegals. The only reason I am giving 3 stars instead of 1 is because of Andrew Kinney, the lawyer who handled my case. Paralegals would receive zero stars- they were that bad at responding and communicating statuses.
